Audrey Hall.age_range: teenager — confirmed not yet eighteen at time of chapter
Audrey Hall.primary_clothing: collared, high-waisted, pale-white dress with engageantes, tight corset, voluminous cage crinoline; white-silk gloves; white dancing shoes with roses and diamonds.
Audrey Hall.face_description: delicate and youthful features, a soft jawline, and large expressive eyes
Audrey Hall.visual_anchor: late teens female, fair skin, long blonde hair, wearing a luxurious Victorian aristocratic dress, holding a cracked bronze mirror
Audrey Hall.distinguishing_features: virid eyes that sparkle with an abnormal radiance; expressive and animated
Audrey Hall.faction: Tarot Club (Justice); noble class resident of Backlund, Empress Borough
Audrey Hall.role: founding member of the Tarot Club, code name Justice
Audrey Hall.source_notes: She picked up a ruby-studded fountain pen to write.

Klein Moretti.eye_color: pitch black (deepened from dark brown to complete darkness)
Klein Moretti.face_description: average-looking features with a rather deep outline; distinct scholarly air; temples show grotesque wound with burn marks in chapter opening scene
Klein Moretti.visual_anchor: early 20s male, brown eyes that turn deep black during divination, holding a silver-inlaid black cane
Klein Moretti.distinguishing_features: graduated from Khoy University; commoner background; four black dots forming a square appeared briefly on the back of his hand after the ritual
Klein Moretti.faction: citizen of Loen Kingdom, Awwa County, City of Tingen; history graduate of Khoy University
Klein Moretti.source_notes: Text explicitly mentions him wearing a tuxedo with an inner pocket for his pocket watch, and carrying a cane.
Klein Moretti.era_clothing_lock: black tuxedo over a white shirt and black vest, paired with trousers, boots, a bow tie, and a silk top hat

Roselle Gustav.face_description: handsome and charismatic face with an air of authority
Roselle Gustav.visual_anchor: middle-aged male, elaborate emperor attire, charismatic and authoritative presence
Roselle Gustav.color_palette: imperial red, gold, white, navy blue
Roselle Gustav.distinguishing_features: Wrote his secret diary using simplified Chinese characters; created secret cards hiding the twenty-two paths of the divine
Roselle Gustav.faction: Emperor; creator of the Blasphemy Slate-derived 22-card system and the tarot cards as a gaming cover
Roselle Gustav.role: historical figure — Intis Republic's Consul, later self-proclaimed Emperor Caesar; inventor of the steam engine, tarot divination, and contemporary card game systems; recognized by the God of Craftsmanship; assassinated in year 1198 of the Fifth Epoch at the White Maple Palace by combined forces of the Church of the Eternal Blazing Sun, the Sauron family (former Intis royal family), and aristocrats
Roselle Gustav.source_notes: Klein recognizes the diary symbols as simplified Chinese characters; Audrey mentions the secret cards.

Alger Wilson.primary_clothing: robe with lightning patterns embroidered on it
Alger Wilson.face_description: Rough face with a stoic and solemn expression.
Alger Wilson.visual_anchor: early 30s male, weathered skin, dark messy hair, wearing a robe embroidered with lightning patterns, holding a quirky-shaped glass bottle
Alger Wilson.color_palette: deep sea blue, silver, dark grey
Alger Wilson.distinguishing_features: unusually dark-blue hair, messy and unkempt
Alger Wilson.accessories: quirky-shaped glass bottle containing supernatural substances — bubbles, frost/snow, and gusting wind visible inside at different moments
Alger Wilson.role: founding member of the Tarot Club, code name The Hanged Man; sailor/seafarer with contacts at Warrior & Sea Bar, Pelican Street, White Rose Borough of Pritz Harbor
Alger Wilson.source_notes: Text explicitly describes his build, hair color, hair style, and clothing.
Alger Wilson.era_clothing_lock: robe embroidered with lightning patterns, worn aboard a three-masted sailboat at sea

Benson Moretti.primary_clothing: linen shirt, black coat, halved top hat
Benson Moretti.face_description: decrepit appearance, resembles Klein but lacks a scholarly air
Benson Moretti.visual_anchor: mid-20s male, pale skin, short black hair, wearing working class Victorian attire, showing signs of fatigue
Benson Moretti.distinguishing_features: literate, employed at an import and export company, resourceful and calculating — negotiated gas pipe installation to avoid debt
Benson Moretti.source_notes: Mentioned tapping his plain cane while inspecting the new house.
Benson Moretti.era_clothing_lock: linen shirt, black coat, halved top hat

Dunn Smith.primary_clothing: white shirt, black vest, black trench coat, hat
Dunn Smith.face_description: Deep gaze, occasionally shows a slight hook at the corners of his lips when showing amusement
Dunn Smith.visual_anchor: mid-aged pale male with slightly high hairline and deep gray eyes, always wearing a black top hat and black knee-length Victorian trench coat, carries a tobacco pipe — codename Nighthawk, Church of Evernight dream-walker
Dunn Smith.distinguishing_features: gray eyes described as deep and hidden in shadow; leads the Nighthawks; references Mandated Punishers, Machinery Hivemind, and other Judiciaries as peer organizations
Dunn Smith.faction: Church of Evernight — Nighthawk Beyonder team
Dunn Smith.source_notes: Text explicitly mentions him picking up his halved top hat and his deep gray eyes.
Dunn Smith.era_clothing_lock: black trench coat and halved top hat

Susie.face_description: Friendly and remarkably intelligent golden retriever face, featuring a soft muzzle and expressive, bright eyes.
Susie.visual_anchor: Intelligent golden retriever dog with lustrous golden fur and bright brown eyes, often sitting with an air of capable assistance.
Susie.color_palette: golden yellow, warm brown, creamy white
Susie.source_notes: Described explicitly as a golden retriever sitting outside the door and referred to as a 'huge dog'.

The Fool.face_description: features hidden by mysterious gray fog
The Fool.visual_anchor: A mysterious male figure shrouded entirely in gray fog, acting as the initiator of the secret Gathering.
The Fool.distinguishing_features: seated at the Seat of Honor at the head of the bronze table in the gray fog palace; identity is Zhou Mingrui (Klein Moretti's body)
The Fool.source_notes: Chapter 6: Zhou Mingrui declares 'You can address me as... The Fool' — confirming The Fool is his chosen identity in the gray fog Tarot world

Justice.face_description: elegant and refined features hidden by fog
Justice.visual_anchor: A slender young woman in an elegant Victorian dress, shrouded in gray fog during the Gathering.
Justice.distinguishing_features: Aura colors of red, purple, blue, and white; emotional aura of red and yellow indicating joy, zeal, and excitement.
Justice.source_notes: Aura and emotional colors observed by Klein using Spirit Vision.

The Hanged Man.face_description: rugged and weathered face hidden by fog
The Hanged Man.visual_anchor: A tall, rugged man in nautical Victorian attire, sitting within the mysterious gray fog.
The Hanged Man.distinguishing_features: Innermost Ether Body/Astral Projection is deep blue, akin to the sea and a tidal storm (visible to Spirit Vision).
The Hanged Man.source_notes: Aura and Astral Projection details observed by Klein using Spirit Vision.
